data.title: Jesco Holdings (TSE:1434) Margin Improvement To 7.2% Tests Earnings Volatility Concerns
data.description:
Jesco Holdings (TSE:1434) has put up another solid quarter, with Q2 2026 revenue of ¥5,829 million and basic EPS of ¥63.08, supported by net income of ¥438 million as the latest set of results lands on investors’ desks. The company has seen revenue move from ¥3,876 million and EPS of ¥39.72 in Q2 2025 to ¥5,829 million and EPS of ¥63.08 in Q2 2026. Trailing twelve month EPS of ¥219.92 and net income of ¥1,526 million provide additional context around the earnings run rate and margin profile. For investors, this earnings print puts the focus squarely on how sustainably Jesco is converting sales into profits and what that means for margins over the next stretch.

See our full analysis for Jesco Holdings.

With the headline numbers in place, the next step is to set these results against the prevailing narratives around Jesco Holdings to see which stories the data supports and which views might need a rethink.

Curious how numbers become stories that shape markets? Explore Community Narratives

Advertisement

75% earnings growth in the last year

  • Trailing 12 month net income sits at ¥1,526 million with basic EPS of ¥219.92, compared with the stated 75% earnings growth rate over the last year and a five year earnings growth pace of 20.1% per year.
  • What stands out for a bullish view is that this 75% earnings growth lines up with higher trailing net income and EPS, which heavily supports the idea of stronger business performance than the longer term 20.1% annual average. However, the pattern of quarterly EPS ranging from a loss in Q3 2025 to ¥104.80 in Q4 2025 and then ¥56.65 and ¥63.08 in Q1 and Q2 2026 is a reminder that results can swing around within that growth story.
    • Supporters pointing to improved profitability can reference the move in trailing net profit margin from 4.9% to 7.2%, alongside the higher trailing EPS of ¥219.92.
    • At the same time, the Q3 2025 loss of ¥32 million and negative EPS of ¥4.61 show that even within a strong year, individual periods have carried project or timing effects that are less friendly to the bullish case.

Margins at 7.2% with a steadier recent run

  • The trailing net profit margin of 7.2%, up from 4.9% the previous year, sits alongside recent quarterly net income of ¥393 million in Q1 2026 and ¥438 million in Q2 2026, with revenue of ¥5,105 million and ¥5,829 million. This shows profits and sales moving together over the last two reported quarters.
  • Supportive for a bullish angle, the 7.2% margin and positive net income across the last four quarters to Q2 2026 align with the higher trailing EPS, yet the earlier Q3 2025 loss of ¥32 million and negative EPS of ¥4.61 keep the focus on how consistently that 7.2% margin can be repeated.
    • Backers of the stronger profitability story can highlight that trailing revenue reached ¥21,314 million against net income of ¥1,526 million, which is consistent with the reported margin step up.
    • On the other hand, the quarterly spread from ¥3,637 million of revenue in Q3 2025 to ¥6,743 million in Q4 2025 shows that volume and mix can move around, which is a useful check on very aggressive bullish expectations around margin stability.

Curious how these margin trends and earnings swings are shaping the bigger picture investors are talking about right now? See what the community is saying about Jesco Holdings.

P/E of 10.4x and price above DCF fair value

  • The stock trades on a trailing P/E of 10.4x, which is below the broader JP market at 14.7x and the JP Construction industry at 12.6x, yet above the peer average of 9.6x. The current share price of ¥2,289 sits above the stated DCF fair value of ¥1,607.03 and alongside a dividend yield of 2.1% and higher share price volatility over the past three months than the JP market.
  • Critics who are cautious on valuation can point to the current price being higher than the DCF fair value and the richer P/E than peers, which matches the idea that recent profit strength and a 2.1% yield are already reflected in the price to some extent, especially when set against the higher recent volatility.
    • Bears highlighting valuation risk can reference the gap between the ¥2,289 share price and the ¥1,607.03 DCF fair value, alongside the peer P/E of 9.6x that sits below Jesco’s 10.4x.
    • At the same time, investors who compare across the market can see that the P/E is still under the JP market at 14.7x and below the 12.6x industry average, so the caution is more about where Jesco sits within its peer group and relative to the DCF number than about an extreme market wide premium.

data.title: Is Highlander Silver’s (TSX:HSLV) Staged Corani Plan Redefining Its Capital and Risk Playbook?
data.description:
• Highlander Silver Corp. recently issued a portfolio update following its completed combination with Bear Creek Mining, outlining active exploration and site-preparation work at the Corani Silver Project in Puno, Peru, including airborne magnetic and induced polarization surveys and the first drilling campaign there in over a decade.
  • A distinctive aspect of the update is the company’s plan to deliver an updated feasibility study using a staged-development approach, supported by experienced Peruvian and international consultants and contractors to help manage capital, funding, timeline and execution risks.
  • Next, we’ll examine how the staged-development feasibility study at Corani could shape Highlander Silver’s investment narrative in the months ahead.

Explore 24 top quantum computing companies leading the revolution in next-gen technology and shaping the future with breakthroughs in quantum algorithms, superconducting qubits, and cutting-edge research.

Advertisement

What Is Highlander Silver's Investment Narrative?

To own Highlander Silver today, you have to believe that folding Corani into the story can justify a high price-to-book multiple and years of losses by eventually turning a zero‑revenue explorer into a producing silver company. The latest update is material for near-term catalysts: it moves Corani from concept to visible execution, with airborne surveys, an active drill program and concrete site works feeding into a staged-development feasibility study due by the end of Q3 2026. That concentrates attention on two things in the months ahead: the quality of exploration and technical results, and how credibly management can frame capital needs after a year of heavy equity issuance. At the same time, the same news also sharpens the biggest risk right now: a capital‑intensive project run by a relatively new board and management team, with no operating cash flow to fall back on if conditions tighten.

However, investors should also weigh how much further dilution might be needed to fund Corani. Our comprehensive valuation report raises the possibility that Highlander Silver is priced higher than what may be justified by its financials.

data.title: Companies Like Hazer Group (ASX:HZR) Can Afford To Invest In Growth
data.description:
There's no doubt that money can be made by owning shares of unprofitable businesses. For example, although software-as-a-service business Salesforce.com lost money for years while it grew recurring revenue, if you held shares since 2005, you'd have done very well indeed. But the harsh reality is that very many loss making companies burn through all their cash and go bankrupt.

So, the natural question for Hazer Group (ASX:HZR) shareholders is whether they should be concerned by its rate of cash burn. For the purposes of this article, cash burn is the annual rate at which an unprofitable company spends cash to fund its growth; its negative free cash flow. The first step is to compare its cash burn with its cash reserves, to give us its 'cash runway'.

AI is about to change healthcare. These 20 stocks are working on everything from early diagnostics to drug discovery. The best part - they are all under $10bn in marketcap - there is still time to get in early.

Advertisement

Does Hazer Group Have A Long Cash Runway?

A cash runway is defined as the length of time it would take a company to run out of money if it kept spending at its current rate of cash burn. When Hazer Group last reported its December 2025 balance sheet in February 2026, it had zero debt and cash worth AU$15m. Looking at the last year, the company burnt through AU$4.3m. Therefore, from December 2025 it had 3.5 years of cash runway. A runway of this length affords the company the time and space it needs to develop the business. The image below shows how its cash balance has been changing over the last few years.

See our latest analysis for Hazer Group

How Is Hazer Group's Cash Burn Changing Over Time?

In the last year, Hazer Group did book revenue of AU$7.3m, but its revenue from operations was less, at just AU$87k. We don't think that's enough operating revenue for us to understand too much from revenue growth rates, since the company is growing off a low base. So we'll focus on the cash burn, today. Notably, its cash burn was actually down by 71% in the last year, which is a real positive in terms of resilience, but uninspiring when it comes to investment for growth. Clearly, however, the crucial factor is whether the company will grow its business going forward. For that reason, it makes a lot of sense to take a look at our analyst forecasts for the company.

How Hard Would It Be For Hazer Group To Raise More Cash For Growth?

There's no doubt Hazer Group's rapidly reducing cash burn brings comfort, but even if it's only hypothetical, it's always worth asking how easily it could raise more money to fund further growth. Issuing new shares, or taking on debt, are the most common ways for a listed company to raise more money for its business. Commonly, a business will sell new shares in itself to raise cash and drive growth. By looking at a company's cash burn relative to its market capitalisation, we gain insight on how much shareholders would be diluted if the company needed to raise enough cash to cover another year's cash burn.

Since it has a market capitalisation of AU$104m, Hazer Group's AU$4.3m in cash burn equates to about 4.1% of its market value. That's a low proportion, so we figure the company would be able to raise more cash to fund growth, with a little dilution, or even to simply borrow some money.

Is Hazer Group's Cash Burn A Worry?

It may already be apparent to you that we're relatively comfortable with the way Hazer Group is burning through its cash. In particular, we think its cash runway stands out as evidence that the company is well on top of its spending. But it's fair to say that its cash burn relative to its market cap was also very reassuring. Taking all the factors in this report into account, we're not at all worried about its cash burn, as the business appears well capitalized to spend as needs be. data.title: Rezolve AI Reopens Commerce.com Talks To Advance AI Native Commerce Vision
data.description:
• Rezolve AI, listed as NasdaqGM:RZLV, has reopened merger talks with Commerce.com after a previously rejected takeover bid.
  • The companies have scheduled a joint investor call to outline plans for a potential AI native commerce platform.
  • This marks a shift from earlier public disputes toward a more collaborative discussion on a possible combination.

Rezolve AI focuses on applying artificial intelligence to commerce, aiming to help merchants and enterprises automate and personalize how customers discover, engage, and transact. The renewed talks with Commerce.com come as many retailers and brands look for technology that can connect data, marketing, and payments into a single, more integrated stack.

For you as an investor, the joint investor call indicates that both sides are at least willing to reengage on deal terms and a shared product vision. Any future agreement, structure, or integration plan remains uncertain. However, the discussion around a full stack AI native commerce platform sets a different tone compared with the earlier rejected bid and public disagreements.

Stay updated on the most important news stories for Rezolve AI by adding it to your watchlist or portfolio. Alternatively, explore our Community to discover new perspectives on Rezolve AI.

1 thing going right for Rezolve AI that this headline doesn't cover.

For Rezolve AI, reopening talks with Commerce.com sits alongside the recent launch of its Brainpowa commerce models on Microsoft Foundry, and together these moves point to a push to sit deeper inside merchants’ tech stacks rather than just selling point solutions. A combination with Commerce.com would, in theory, pair Rezolve’s AI-native tooling with an existing merchant base and payments rails. The Microsoft tie up gives large enterprises a way to test and deploy commerce copilots within Azure, Dynamics 365 and Microsoft 365. For you, the key question is whether these partnerships translate into contract wins and usage that support Rezolve AI’s previously outlined growth ambitions, or whether integration risk, deal complexity, and reliance on partners slow that down. Investors also need to weigh prior public friction between Rezolve AI and Commerce.com’s board, including a poison pill, against the current more collaborative tone and ask whether governance and deal terms can align in a way that is sustainable for both shareholder groups.

Advertisement

How This Fits Into The Rezolve AI Narrative

  • The potential merger with Commerce.com lines up with Rezolve AI’s narrative of becoming a consolidator in AI commerce, using acquisitions and channel partners to broaden distribution and deepen recurring revenue across retail clients.
  • However, the earlier failed bid, governance disputes, and Commerce.com’s use of a poison pill highlight that integration is not guaranteed, which pushes against the narrative’s assumption of smooth roll up execution and fast cross sell into a unified platform.
  • The Brainpowa deployment on Microsoft Foundry, and the idea of a full stack smart commerce platform with Commerce.com, add a layer of partner and ecosystem exposure that the narrative only partially reflects, especially around reliance on large cloud providers and third party merchant networks.

Knowing what a company is worth starts with understanding its story. Check out one of the top narratives in the Simply Wall St Community for Rezolve AI to help decide what it's worth to you.

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

  • ⚠️ Analysts have flagged 3 key risks for Rezolve AI, including ongoing losses and recent shareholder dilution, and a complex merger process with Commerce.com could add execution and financing pressure if terms are not clearly aligned.
  • ⚠️ Rezolve AI already leans on large partners such as Microsoft for distribution, so layering on a Commerce.com combination would further concentrate the business on a few external platforms and governance structures that Rezolve AI does not fully control.
  • 🎁 If the renewed talks progress to a deal on improved terms, combining Rezolve AI’s commerce specific AI models with Commerce.com’s merchant network could strengthen the use case against larger competitors like Shopify, Salesforce, and Adobe in enterprise retail.
  • 🎁 The availability of Brainpowa inside Microsoft Foundry, together with a possible Commerce.com tie up, gives Rezolve AI multiple touchpoints with global retailers, which may increase the chance that new products and features can be rolled out across existing channels rather than built from scratch each time.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, focus on what Rezolve AI and Commerce.com actually disclose on the investor call, especially around deal structure, governance, integration timelines, and how a combined platform would be packaged and sold to merchants. Clear milestones around pilot deployments, contracted revenue tied to the AI-native commerce offering, and evidence that Brainpowa usage on Microsoft Azure is translating into paid, production workloads will matter more than broad vision statements. Also keep an eye on any fresh capital plans or dilution tied to a potential transaction, and on how competitors in commerce focused AI respond with their own partnerships or product launches.

To ensure you're always in the loop on how the latest news impacts the investment narrative for Rezolve AI, head to the community page for Rezolve AI to never miss an update on the top community narratives.
